title: Multi-Stage Procedure Exceeds Original Consent Boundary
scenario: A patient consents to the first stage of a treatment plan, but a later stage is attempted without renewed or extended consent.
initial_state: Consent was valid for the initial stage of care.
state_transition: The workflow advances to a later stage outside the original consent boundary.
execution_boundary: The later-stage procedure is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity must cover the specific stage of care being executed.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the original consent does not extend to the later-stage action.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when staged care exceeds consent boundaries.
clinical_action: Execute later-stage treatment without extended consent
